clues.clue: Right, there's a woman who ties things. Professional tier, she is. Ties knots for a living. And she's just landed somewhere. Tie-land.
A load of balls. Literally. Someone's just dropped a box of small round balls all over the floor. Ball-ee. Everywhere.
Jay's got some jam. He's standing there in May. Jay, jam, May. He's having a lovely time. Sort of.
A woman called Martina is in a queue for something. She's very particular. She wants it, but she wants it unique. Caribbean island.
Right, something belongs to someone, his, it's his, and there's a small frying pan involved, and a woman called Viola who's dropped the V. Caribbean island, shared between two countries.
A group of people are describing a stretch of land as being free. They keep saying it's free. And there's a crow flying about making that ka sound. South Asia.
Something has viciously attacked a bloke who was just stood there in his best outfit. Completely unprovoked. He's still wearing it, to be fair.
A spider has gone somewhere it really shouldn't. Very dim in there. It's not coming back out and it won't say what it found.
A pair of things, just two of them, have been to a factory and come out having multiplied. There were two going in and considerably more coming out.
Your mum's gone all legal. Very firm on the law, she is, and she's agreeing with absolutely everything.
Something you say firmly when you mean it, the opposite of yes, and a small buzzing creature has got itself mixed up in the whole thing. Going ooh about it.
That wandering woman, always off somewhere, but she always puts the letter R at the front of her name. On everything.
Right, you're digging around. Underground mainly. Looking for stuff. And then you build things out of it. That's basically it.
She keeps getting taken. Every time. You'd think they'd have sorted the security by now. It's become a bit of a pattern.
That fella went at full speed into a crate. Just ran straight into it. Sort of his thing.
A woman called Chichi has been sizing people up at the entrance to somewhere in Mexico. Very intense. She keeps saying 'it-za' like she's about to sneeze.
A fella called Taj has found himself on a very big hill and he's done something wrong. His ma is not best pleased. Very marble. Very India.
A man with his arms wide open has annoyed a Christian fella called Chris. The whole thing has turned into a scene. Very dramatic. Very Brazilian.
Right, someone's been at that tree trunk with an axe, and when they were done they went, yeah alright, you can have it. Generous.
There's a bloke called Pete and he's got a small amount of that cooking liquid, and he's come in looking useful. Like an implement.
Rich bloke from Wales, right, and there's a town nearby. Small town. He's staring at it like it means something.
That lad who's always banging on things - real knocker - he's moved into a cottage deep in the trees. Hammering away in the forest.
That royal residence, right - big, impressive - it's been built entirely out of frozen water. Spectacular, mind you.
That lad's gone radiant - just glowing - and he's moved to the coast. Very lit up, the whole town is.
A bloke called Matt has gone and done something to the west side of something. Quite aggressive about it. He's done it in the middle of a historical drama and no one seems bothered.
Someone has bought a turban and decided it needs a bit of a wash. They've given it a proper rinse. It's come out a bit greenish. Nobody's surprised.
A bloke called Carl has found a lion who's in a bit of a state. The lion is making a noise like it needs something. Carl's keeping his distance. Wisely.
A fella called Sear has gone to a town called Tone-in. Very small place. Not on many maps. He seems happy there. Something to do with mood.
A bloke called Tess has got a proper big stone and he's carrying it around with great determination.
A photo has been taken by someone with an unusual name and it's come out all green. Something about light and plants.
Right, that thing where you slam your teeth together - really hard - and something shatters. Bit violent. Done it with toast before.
There's a fella called Dime who's doing a bit of scaffolding. Just the one bar. Standing there.
Someone's saying something very quietly. Barely got the energy to speak up. Just sort of... murmuring. Near enough.
Right, someone's given a child genius a little poke to get their attention. A prod, like. The kid's apparently very gifted.
A woman called Amy's aiming at something, she's lined it up, very focused, and behind her there's a house full of wine bottles. Bit of a state.
It's that letter A, right, and then someone's said the word 'dell' after it. Like a small valley. Together. Say it out loud. There you go.
A lad called Ed has gone inside somewhere and he keeps appearing at the edge of things. Not quite in the main bit. Just around the fringes. He's at the periphery but he seems alright about it.
A small silly noise has been made by someone's dad in a very casual way near a toilet abroad. He seems very relaxed. Nobody knows quite what he said. He said it confidently though.
A famous Japanese mountain has been having a bit of a moment with some very loud music. The mountain itself isn't playing. It's just standing there being volcanic while bands do their thing nearby.
There's a woman called Sarah and she's gone and rung the bell at him. Just stood there and rang it. Didn't say anything. Bell him, Sarah.
Someone's made a small vehicle entirely out of clay. Just sat there on the table. Bit of an odd project but there you go.
Right, they've gone and dyed a picture frame. Whole thing. Didn't need doing but someone's gone ahead and done it anyway.
Right, a small French bloke has only gone and made a book about tyres. Wrote the whole thing. Very comprehensive.
Someone at the market has gone and borrowed a load of women. Not sure why. Just borrowed them. A load of them.
There's a fella at the back of a queue and he's gone all wobbly and smooth. Very liquidy now. Nice.
That male deer, right, he's been going at it - bucking away - and he's brought home a great big posh house. Bit over the top.
Easy one. The breeze, it's got two choices here, and one of them is a big old fort up on a hill. Alright?
Old Roman emperor, right, he's had a bit of a barrier put up. Keeping things out. Can't argue with that.
That lad from Germany, right, he's trying to tell you something but you're not listening. He keeps trying.
Right, you've got four of something, each one slightly more annoying than the last. Someone's been breeding them.
That Scottish bloke's got a metal bar where his eyebrow is. Bit alarming really.
Right, there's a fella and his back is completely bare. No shirt on. And he's saying something. Just calling out. That's it.
Some fella's come round again. Had a good rest and now he's back. Refreshed. Done it twice, mind.
A fella called Abe has gone and got himself a bit of a shrine. People are visiting it. It's quite big. Bit of a monument.
In Southeast Asia, someone has gone for a long wander and ended up eating something halfway through. Very much on the go. They're still chewing. The river doesn't care, it just keeps moving.

In Iraq, someone has spotted a very old river and said, that one's got his name on it. Very possessive. The tiger in question was not consulted. It just flows on, quietly ignoring the whole dispute.
Right, a woman called Hannah's got herself a little vehicle. Not sure why she needs one but there it is.
Someone's having a yawn over a smoked fish. Bit rude. The fish hasn't done anything wrong.
There's a V-shape and someone's put their bag on it. Bit of an odd place to leave a bag.
That fella's settling up a debt with his mate - his good pal - and handing over some cash. Very wholesome.
Right, there's a little candle - the waxy thing you light in a church - and it's gone and, erm, done something to you. Near enough.
Easy one this. Someone's read something - past tense, they've gone through the whole thing - and now they're giving everyone a full update on it.
Small copper coin, yeah. One penny. And it's sitting on a road. A quiet little road. Not a motorway. Just a lane. That's it.
It's a thing. That's all I know. Not nothing, not everything. Just a thing. Some kind of thing. I can't explain it. There it is.
Right, you've stopped fighting it. You're not forcing anything. Whatever's going to happen will happen. You're just... allowing it. Going with the flow.
There's a place in the Middle East - old city, oranges and that - and it's gone and baked a load of round spongy things. Nice.
There's a venue - the kind with loud music and coloured lights - and it's got one of those hard dunking biscuits inside. Unusual venue policy.
A woman called Gwen, just sitting there, holding a pen. Looking at it. That's your lot.
A bloke called Trap has been out to sea in a small boat. He's come back with four sides but two of them are parallel. Says it's fine.
A diamond-shaped fella from the housing estate, everyone round there knows him, has got four equal sides and he's not apologising for any of them.
Someone's been told they've got many terms to deal with. Multiple. Could be any number really. They don't look thrilled about it.
A bloke called Simon's got a mobile phone. He's decided to ring it. That's the whole thing.
Right, there's a bloke who's been pulling things. On a rope. Three in a row. Getting tired by the third one.
Some woman's had a card delivered. Big one. From her son. She's a bit tearful about it. Happy though.
There's a long line of fellas just standing there waiting. All shuffling forward. No one's happy about it. What a queue, man.
Right, the fella at the surgery has been acting very odd all morning. Bit peculiar. Patients are looking at each other going, what's got into him.
Someone's left one of them outdoor lamps out on the patio all winter and it's gone a funny colour. Sort of minty. Not ideal.
Someone's dipped into something quite heavy, a real load of it, and then there's a bloke swearing at the end of the whole business. Very undignified situation.
A lizard's been on a long journey and at the end of it he's met a bloke called Don. The lizard hasn't said much. Don's been doing all the talking.
A bloke called Archie has gone off on a boat, a proper biblical one, and then he's chosen something, but it all ends very suddenly with a sharp sound. Archie seems pleased.
A fish has got breathing equipment fitted on its side and someone from Bavaria has pointed at it going 'der'. Like they're explaining something obvious.
A small animal, a cat or a dog, something like that, has said something out loud and then thanked you for listening. Very polite.
A person who makes things up, can't help themselves, just says whatever suits them, has gone and done it again. No surprise there.
Someone's been sat in the sun for a bit too long and now they need to get moving. Got a bit of colour. Time to leave.
There's a bloke called Sam and he's doing an impression of a sheep. Not a great one. Very short. Just the one noise.
Right, someone at the cricket's doing the thing where they send the ball down the pitch. Nice and round, the arm. Doing it properly.
A bloke in China with a very old-fashioned name, the kind you'd have in Peking, has turned up with a bird tucked under his arm. Thin pancakes as well.
A stew from North Africa has arrived on a train wearing a pointy hat. It got on at Casablanca. Very fragrant journey.
A Mexican bloke called Nacho has made something cheesy and crispy and covered it in stuff. He's named it after himself. Fair enough.
It's someone's go next - their turn - and a man called Rick has only gone and merged into something. Just blended himself in.
A donkey has fetched something - gone and got it - and there's a woman called Ida waiting for it at the other end. The donkey's done the fetch. Ida's just standing there.
There's a lemon. It's on the grass. That's it. Someone's just left a lemon on the grass and walked off. Nobody's explained why.
Someone's taken a swing at a small pointy thing you hammer into wood. Gone straight for it.
A woman's completely failed to board a vessel. It's gone without her. She's still on the harbour. Gutted.
Right, someone's settled themselves on top of a garden barrier. Can't decide which way to go. Just sitting there.
Right, that vase you keep a dead person's ashes in - it's just been sat there doing nothing all afternoon. Not moved.
There's a gran who's been in a bit of drama. Not a full play, just a bit of a scene. She handled it, mind.
That sport where they go at it with the curved sticks - the one on ice - well, he decided to have a go at it. Just got on with it.
There's a man called Lester, and he's gone absolutely crimson. Bright red. Don't know what happened but he's not well.
A massive weight - a full imperial tonne - it hasn't moved. Been there ages. Just sitting there, perfectly still. Nobody's bothered.
Morrissey and Ella Fitzgerald are the same person. That's what they're saying. Moz is Ella.
There's been a conflict, right, and the pig at the middle of it all just won't leave. Stubborn animal.
Saying hello to a lady called Ena. Very politely and that.
Right, a pot that's been left out in the open, it's gone all wild. Just standing there being feral.
Someone's had a bit of a mishap with their instrument (the stringed one, not a piano) and now there's a mask of it. All red and saucy looking.
That place in Ukraine, capital city and that, it's gone and had a bird move in. Very buttery situation apparently.
There's a bloke called Norman who runs a corner shop. Very calm fella. Never stressed. Does a lovely curry.
Right, that long thin stick you use for fishing's been left in the hot broth. And some bloke's made it into a painting. Bit odd.
You have to go and steal some of them long orange vegetables. Just nip in and take them. That's all.
The letter L's got a very heavy weight attached to it, and it's parked next to a toilet. Don't ask.
A Greek woman has a jar of yoghurt and a cucumber and she's getting properly stuck in. There's a goat nearby just watching. The goat seems fine with the whole situation.
A bloke in Provence has seen something and gone, right, I'll have some of that. He's saying it in a very French way. There's garlic and oil involved. He's very confident about the garlic.
Someone in North Africa has been having a right row. Properly going for it. Very fiery about the whole thing. They've put the row into a jar and labelled it. It's quite red.
Someone's measured the exact middle of a road with a very expensive tape measure. And they've written it down. Very pleased with themselves.
Right, there's this road and at the end of it there's another road, and at the end of that one there's a bloke called Ron. And he's ranged all along it.
Someone called Nish is kneeling in a field going through a big bunch of keys. He's looking for one specific one. Won't say which.

A woman called Cheryl has gone to a place in the Ukraine. She's had a good look round. She's bought a postcard. She's not been able to post it because the post office has been shut for about forty years.
A big lad called Titus has been on a boat. He said it was unsinkable. His mate Ian said that was tempting fate. Titus said it'd be fine. It wasn't fine.

A fella called Colin has gone on a trip to South America. Not on a plane. In a rocket. Everyone watching said it'd go fine. Colin said it'd go fine. It didn't go fine. There's a river named after the place he was heading.
There's a woman - your mum, sort of - and she keeps saying this thing. Over and over. The same sound. Coming out with it repeatedly.
Right, there's a saucepan. And someone's arrived. And they seem quite pleased about both of those things. That's the gist of it.
A mate of yours is alarmed - and his name's very holy-sounding - and he's been polishing something till it shines. Made it sparkle. He's very pleased.

The letter that makes the dee sound has gone on holiday to somewhere sheltered from the wind (very calm there, protected) and when it got back it had become a blacksmith. Made a few horseshoes. Seemed pleased.

A female horse has come in and it's gone a bit red in the face, embarrassed about something. And there's a small round fruit (soft, grows on a bush) sitting there looking very smug about the whole situation.

A bloke who wrote a lot of letters to people, very important one, has gone for a walk into a forest but the trees aren't normal trees. They've got bright red berries and pointed leaves. He seems fine with this.
Right, there's a massive fella. Enormous. Been asleep on the path blocking everything. Can't be moved. Just there, snoring. Blocking the whole road.
There's a big scary thing living in a lake. Massive. Like a proper sea monster but it went to the gym. Made itself enormous and terrifying.
Right, there's a woman - old fashioned name, sounds like a lamp post - and she's been on a boat. A luxury cruise. Very graceful. Bit like a dinosaur in the water.
A bloke from Manchester called Stan has gone missing during a mango-eating competition. Nobody's too bothered. His brother says it happens every summer.
A woman from California has gone to a forum. Not online. An actual forum. Like the Romans had. She's asked a question and everyone's having a proper debate about it.
Someone's potted a plant and it's done well. Really well. Suspiciously well. The neighbours are a bit jealous about it. They've been asking what he put in the soil and he's not telling.
A lad's said something, right, and it was an order, and then he just went off and did his laundry. That's his whole day.
Someone's landed their little fishing net right on top of a piece of music. It's just sitting there on the stave.
That lad Ian's checking something out, then he's putting it in a line. Just queuing things up all day.
Right, the king of all the land - proper royalty - but he's a terrible fibber. Can't tell the truth to save himself. The lying king. Say it out loud.
A wall. Solid wall, nice brickwork. And right next to it, someone's put the letter E. Just the letter, leaning on the wall. That's your lot.
There's a bloke called Moe. And he's with a woman called Ana. Moe and Ana. That's all there is to it.
There's a magazine - a glossy one - and it's saying no, and then someone's telling a lie. All happening at once, which is a lot.
Right, there's a bloke called Doug and he's found himself a young Scottish lass and she's wearing a fur coat. Don't know where they met.
One of those primates - the smaller ones, not gorillas - has gone and bought itself a jigsaw. Proper stumped by it.
He's got a bag, right, and he's chucking out everything in it. Brand new container. Empty.
There's less of him than before, and somehow he came out on top. Sort of works.
That big pub at the end of the road, everything's there except the letters. It's just the eye and the building.
There is something coming off the big blazing thing in the sky. Invisible but it is out there. Very fast.
Right, the orange stuff under the surface. Between the rocky outside and the hot middle bit. Very thick.
That twisty ladder thing inside every single one of your cells. Has all your information on it. Like a filing system.
There's a face covering, right, and it belongs to someone specific. That's your clue.
The cooking fat's gone all runny because it warmed up. Left it on the hob too long.
The path up to the house is absolutely covered in stones. Not maintained at all.
There's a bloke called Mo, right, and he's absolutely roasting in the heat. Proper sweating. Then he makes a disappointed noise.
Someone's clearing a floor with a bucket and they've got the thing attached to a stick - you know the thing - and it's a bit lit up, and there's a man. All happening at the same time.
Right, there's a fella called Harvey and he's only gone and headbutted a partition. Straight into it. What's he done that for?
Right, there's this island, and everyone on it keeps saying 'sigh' like they're really bored. Cyprus. Not bored, just the sound.
That woman's found a small map in her bag. She's like, me nor-ca, I've got me nor-ca. It's a map, love.
A sharp point on a fence, barbed wire type of arrangement, and there's two of them. A barb and dos of them.
We all want a bit more of something but it's making us feel a bit poorly. There's a group of us involved and we're all in the same boat. Indian Ocean.
A woman is standing by the sea saying the word for a hard outer casing of a sea creature over and over. Just repeating it. Calmly. Indian Ocean again.
There's a pub, a proper one, and someone with a very unusual name has opened it. Zanzi, they're called. Not sure where that's from. East Africa somewhere.
A bloke called John, definitely from Troy, he's been very clear about that, has turned up on a horse. He won't say what's inside it.
Nothing has happened today. Absolutely nothing. Someone's kept a record of it. Full log. Completely empty.
A film came out, a sequel to a previous one, and it went straight to the doctor for a jab. Didn't even wait for the reviews.
Right, that Phili has gone and got himself a pine. A tall tree. Phili-pine. And there's more than one. Phili-pines. Plural and everything.
That bloke Indone has gone and found himself something. Classic. He seems very happy with it.
Your mum's gone completely flat out, just lying there, and there's a small island next to her.
She's off again. Into another ruin. Probably shouldn't be in there but that's never stopped her.
Two fighters. And it's not pretty. Very loud. Someone shouts something dramatic at the end of it all.
Right, there's a bear with a bird in his backpack. They're best mates. Off on some sort of musical adventure. Bit mad.
A pet called Peter has gone to a shop (a very rocky one, carved into a cliff face) and come back with something. In Jordan. He went through a narrow gap to get there. Didn't explain why.
A bloke has gone a bit quiet (not quite an angry quiet, more of a thoughtful quiet) and he's now sitting in a very large temple in Cambodia. Something's caught his attention.
A group of blokes have rolled up some very heavy rocks in England, right out in a field, and then arranged them in a circle. Nobody knows why. The rocks are not saying.
Right, a fella called Ken has got a copy. Not sure what of. He just has one.
There's loads of supplies - tins and that - but the bloke's keeping them all at his house. Won't share.
That little round thing you put on a pizza or a Greek salad - it's going a bit slow. Take its time.
Someone's gone and put a microphone in the middle of an angel. Wedged it right in.
The wine bottle got sent off in a vehicle and it drove away. Gone now.
There's a soothing cream on the door and someone applies it to the door every single day without fail.
That plant in the garden, the one that survives frost, it's also got a bloke called Tom's name on it somehow. Resilient plant, is Tom.
Right, I've carved a walking stick out of coal. That black fuel stuff. It's mine, this stick. My coal walking aid.
Emma's at the door and Tom's lad is right behind her. Come in, both of you.
A marine has come up from the sea and there's been some argument about whether the water was actually a marine thing or just a general water thing. The marine seems annoyed.
Pete has had a dot. Just the one dot. Stuck it on something. He's quite pleased with it. Small, round, very green. Pete's done his bit.
There's a tan being done in a country. The whole country is getting a tan, very systematically. Someone's timing it. The tan is coming out quite nice actually.
My toe is in a state. It's got a condition, something wrong with it, and it's been going on for a while. My toe. Con-dry-a.
A fella called Chris has gone to a place in Rome where they vote. He's in there now. Voting away. Very civic-minded.
A fella called Adren has lined up at the starting blocks. The gun goes off. He's gone. He's absolutely legged it. Very fast man.
Right, that thing where you go up to a counter and pay - someone's done it wrong and ended up slightly bent out of shape. That's all.
Some fellas are being quite clever about things. Bit too smart, if you ask me. Think they know everything.
That bloke's gone all twisty down at the gym. Like a pretzel but he used to be straight. Bit weird.

Half beer, half lemonade, and it's got a name. Not a famous name. Just a name. Bass, as it goes.

Someone's had a bit of a wobble right in the middle of their workout. Light went out. Didn't finish the set.
That bloke from Nazareth's gone and got himself a little vehicle. Nothing fancy. Just rolling about.
You're out west somewhere. Everything's going brilliantly. And there's a massive horse. Just standing there, looking impressive.
There's a bit of fabric, the edge of it, just a little one. And someone's made a small clearing in the woods near an old farmhouse.
This person has been roused from their sleep. Had a proper awakening. And now they're still at it, going on about it.
A man called Tag has just been told off in a very long, drawn-out way. Someone's literally laid into him. And then a woman called Ellie turned up at the end. Don't know why.

A man called Ling has only gone and spoken in a very long, flat, drawn-out way. Like he's reading from a very thin script. Edge to edge.

There's a fella who's done away with another fella using a ceramic pot. Not sure why he had it out for the pot, but there you go.
Right, it's very important that someone has a wash. The whole thing hinges on it. Can't stress it enough.
That bloke could've been a toilet. Literally built for that purpose. But he wasn't. Still walks about though.
A jay bird - the blue one, a bit aggressive with the other birds - has had a son. A baby jay. A son. That's it.
She was made - like they made her, past tense - and her name's Marian. Mary's little sister type of name. Marian. They made her.
Right, there's a king - a regal, proper king - and he's been doing some paintings. Very artistic king. And there's a woman's sound at the end.
Someone jumped out at a fella and he just sort of... stared back. Nothing. Like it was obvious.
Right, a wise teacher said no to something. Quite firmly. Had the knack of it as well.
There's a bloke called Al and someone's been watching him. Following him about. And this Al, right, he's in quite good shape.
Right, there's a zoo keeper and he's responsible for one specific animal. Just the one. Him. That animal.
That rope's gone all saggy and loose. Just hanging there with no tension in it whatsoever. Completely limp.
Someone's letting go of a cardboard container - just releasing it and watching it fall. That's their afternoon sorted.
Some steps going up a wall, right. And at the top of them there's paradise. A really brilliant place. Lovely up there.
There's an odour, isn't there. A whiff. And it's coming from a younger person. And hanging round them there's some sort of invisible energy force. Sort of ghostly.
He's off and running, this fella. Proper momentum. And you're not to get in his way. Don't even think about slowing him down. Just stand aside.
That rough scratchy thing that gets stuck to your woolly jumper when you walk through a field - it's sat on top of a bread roll. Bizarre.
Right, that yellow wobbly pudding stuff your nan makes - it's gone and joined forces with the thick white dairy stuff. Together now.
There's a Gary - just a normal Gary - he's got a ball in his hand and he's wandered off somewhere humming. Dee, he's going.
An ancient Greek bloke has turned up with a set of triangles and he's very smug about the long side. Won't stop going on about it.
There's a wooden cabin in a forest and someone's doing a sum in there. Very slow sum. Took most of the afternoon.
A nun called Hippa has gone on a bit of a mission. She's crossing the longest side of a triangle and she's going to be a while.
That fella's agreed to do something. Nodded along to it. Said he was in. Can't back out now.
